message:
fix(scanner): rescue cass-target* from cass-* prefix shadow

Fresh-eyes review of the previous patch (107e7ee) caught a regression:
the new `cass-prefix-hyphen` pattern (Prefix("cass-"), 0.86, AgentWorkspace)
shadowed the pre-existing `cass-target` pattern (Prefix("cass-target"),
0.82, RustTarget) because classify() picks the highest-confidence match.

For names like `cass-target-thatlilac`:
  before: classified RustTarget, structural_score 0.92-0.98 with cargo markers
  after 107e7ee (regression): AgentWorkspace, flat 0.78 — drops below the
    deletion confidence floor on machines without populated cargo signals
  after this fix: RustTarget restored

Bumped `cass-target` confidence to 0.94 (above the new 0.86 cass-prefix
patterns). Both still RustTarget; the more specific name wins so stats
group consistently and cargo-marker structural boost still applies.

The misleading test comment that claimed `cass-target-thatlilac` was
"already covered by cass-target" was technically false after 107e7ee —
the assertion only checked category != Unknown, so the regression slipped
through. Replaced with a dedicated regression test asserting both the
category AND the specific pattern_name match.

All 1871 lib tests pass; cargo fmt clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
